title: Rich Velvety Moroccan Soya Bean Tagine
description: A flavorful and aromatic Moroccan dish featuring soya beans in a rich and velvety sauce, perfect for a brunch mood. This vegetarian-friendly recipe is made with a blend of spices and herbs, giving it a unique and delicious taste.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large comal over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the ground cumin, coriander, turmeric, and smoked paprika, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the soaked and drained soya beans, whole grain mustard, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add 2 cups of water to the comal and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the soya beans are tender.
Use an immersion blender to puree the sauce to a rich and velvety consistency.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
